title: MobX
objectives: Compare MobX's auto-tracking to explicit approaches

sections.markdown: MobX is a reactive state library built on three concepts: observables (tracked state), actions (mutate state), and reactions (auto-run when observed state changes). It tracks dependencies transparently.

MobX's magic is transparent reactive tracking: an Observer rebuilds automatically when any observable it READ during build changes — you don't wire up listeners manually. This minimizes boilerplate but relies on code generation.
Mutating observables outside an @action (warnings/inconsistency) or expecting Observer to rebuild for state it didn't read during build. Read the observable inside the Observer's builder.
MobX feels natural to developers from a Vue/MobX-JS background. It needs build_runner codegen; weigh that against Riverpod/BLoC which Flutter teams adopt more widely today.

flashcards.back: Observables (tracked state), actions (mutations), reactions (auto-run on change).

flashcards.front: How does Observer know to rebuild?
flashcards.back: It tracks which observables were read during build and rebuilds when they change.

flashcards.front: What is a computed in MobX?
flashcards.back: A cached derived value recomputed only when its dependencies change.

quiz.q: MobX state that is tracked is called an…

options: An observable it read during build changes
quiz.explain: Tracking is based on what was read during build.

challenges.text: Add a @computed derived value and display it.
Use a reaction to trigger side effects (e.g., persist on change).

interviewQuestions: Explain MobX observables, actions and reactions.
How does transparent reactive tracking work?
